query_normalized: investigating the influence of network topology on spontaneous brain activity patterns neuroscience
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b): it measures spontaneous brain activity patterns (resting state fMRI, low-frequency BOLD fluctuations) in the neuroscience domain, which directly corresponds to the dependent variable in the user's question. Functional connectivity correlation patterns ARE the standard operationalization of network topology in neuroscience literature, even though the paper uses "correlation patterns" and "functional connectivity" terminology rather than "network topology
